Primary Use: Evaluating cranial base symmetry, condylar angulation, and transverse discrepancies from a bottom-up angle.
Providing a broad overview of the dentition, jaws, and vertical ramal/condylar asymmetries.
Evaluating the lateral aspect of the TMJ and condylar position/translation.
Providing a clear view of the condylar head and neck across the pharynx.
Viewing the TMJ from an anterior-to-posterior perspective through the orbit.
Examining the midface, maxillary sinuses, and zygomatic arches.
Evaluating the frontal and ethmoid sinuses, as well as lateral orbital walls.
Checking for medial/lateral condylar displacement and evaluating the occipital bone.
Assessing skeletal bone age and pubertal growth stages.
A single 3D volumetric scan that digitally recreates 2D views without superimposition or magnification.
